title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR DETECTING BORDER APPEARANCE DEFECTS OF ELECTRONIC DEVICE; 电子设备边框外观瑕疵检测方法及设备; PROCÉDÉ ET DISPOSITIF POUR DÉTECTER DES DÉFAUTS D'ASPECT DE BORDURE D'UN DISPOSITIF ÉLECTRONIQUE
abstract:
The objective of the present invention is to provide a method and device for detecting border appearance defects of an electronic device. In the present invention, an appearance image of an electronic device is acquired; a border appearance region image of the electronic device is extracted from the appearance image of the electronic device, and the border appearance region image is adjusted to be an image that has the same length and width; the adjusted border appearance region image is inputted into a trained model of an FPN network combined with a backbone network; an outputted defect detection result of a border appearance region of the electronic device is received from the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network, the defect detection result comprising: the defect type of the border of the electronic device, the position of a defect in the border of the electronic device, and the confidence level of the defect detection result, so that defect differences in the border appearance of second-hand electronic devices such as mobile phone electronic devices may be accurately identified.

L'objectif de l'invention est de fournir un procédé et un dispositif permettant de détecter les défauts d'aspect de bordure d'un dispositif électronique. Selon l'invention, une image d'aspect d'un dispositif électronique est acquise ; une image de zone d'aspect de bordure du dispositif électronique est extraite de l'image d'aspect du dispositif électronique, puis l'image de zone d'aspect de bordure est ajustée de façon à obtenir une image ayant la même longueur et la même largeur ; l'image de zone d'aspect de bordure ajustée est entrée dans un modèle appris d'un réseau FPN combiné à un réseau fédérateur ; un résultat de détection de défaut émis d'une zone d'aspect de bordure du dispositif électronique est reçu du modèle du réseau FPN combiné au réseau fédérateur, le résultat de détection de défaut comprenant le type de défaut de la bordure du dispositif électronique, la position d'un défaut dans la bordure du dispositif électronique, ainsi que le niveau de confiance du résultat de détection de défaut, de façon à pouvoir identifier précisément les différences de défaut dans l'aspect de bordure des dispositifs électroniques de seconde main, tels que des dispositifs électroniques de téléphones mobiles.

本发明的目的是提供一种电子设备边框外观瑕疵检测方法及设备,本发明通过获取电子设备的外观图像;从所述电子设备的外观图像中提取该电子设备的边框外观区域图像,将所述边框外观区域图像调整为长度和宽度相同的图像;将调整后的边框外观区域图像输入训练结束后的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型;从所述FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型接收输出的电子设备的边框外观区域的瑕疵检测结果,所述瑕疵检测结果包括:电子设备的边框的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在电子设备的边框中的位置和瑕疵检测结果的置信度,能够准确地识别二手电子设备如手机的电子设备边框外观的瑕疵差异。
claims:
一种电子设备边框外观瑕疵检测方法,其中,该方法包括: 获取电子设备的外观图像; 从所述电子设备的外观图像中提取该电子设备的边框外观区域图像,将所述边框外观区域图像调整为长度和宽度相同的图像; 将调整后的边框外观区域图像输入训练结束后的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型; 从所述FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型接收输出的电子设备的边框外观区域的瑕疵检测结果,所述瑕疵检测结果包括:电子设备的边框的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在电子设备的边框中的位置和瑕疵检测结果的置信度。 根据权利要求1所述的方法,其中,从所述电子设备的外观图像中提取该电子设备的边框外观区域图像,包括: 采用Unet实例分割的方式,从所述电子设备的外观图像中提取该电子设备的边框外观区域图像。 根据权利要求1所述的方法,其中,所述backbone网络的前2层采用res结构,网络的后2层采用inception结构。 根据权利要求1所述的方法,其中,从所述FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型接收输出的电子设备的边框外观区域的瑕疵检测结果之后,还包括: 识别所述瑕疵检测结果的置信度是否大于第一预设阈值,若大于所述第一预设阈值,则输出包括电子设备的边框的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在电子设备的边框中的位置的结果信息。 根据权利要求1所述的方法,其中,将所述边框外观区域图像输入FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型之前,还包括: 步骤一,预设FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型及其初始的模型参 数; 步骤二,将样本电子设备的边框外观区域图像输入带有当前的模型参数的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型,得到样本电子设备的边框的瑕疵预测结果,所述瑕疵预测结果包括:样本电子设备的边框的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在样本电子设备的边框中的位置和瑕疵检测结果的置信度; 步骤三,基于预设目标函数计算所述瑕疵预测结果与样本电子设备的真实瑕疵结果之间的差值,识别所述差值是否大于第二预设阈,若所述差值大于第二预设阈值,则步骤四,基于所述差值更新所述FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型参数后,重新从步骤二开始执行; 若所述差值小于等于第二预设阈值,则步骤五,将带有当前的模型参数的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型作为训练结束后的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型。 根据权利要求1所述的方法,其中,将所述边框外观区域图像调整为长度和宽度相同的图像,包括: 将所述边框外观区域图像的长度方向进行缩放,并将所述边框外观区域图像的宽度方向进行填充。 一种电子设备边框外观瑕疵检测设备,其中,该设备包括: 第一装置,用于获取电子设备的外观图像; 第二装置,用于从所述电子设备的外观图像中提取该电子设备的边框外观区域图像,将所述边框外观区域图像调整为长度和宽度相同的图像; 第三装置,用于将调整后的边框外观区域图像输入训练结束后的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型; 第四装置,用于从所述FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型接收输出的电子设备的边框外观区域的瑕疵检测结果,所述瑕疵检测结果包括:电 子设备的边框的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在电子设备的边框中的位置和瑕疵检测结果的置信度。 根据权利要求7所述的装置,其中,所述第二装置,用于采用Unet实例分割的方式,从所述电子设备的外观图像中提取该电子设备的边框外观区域图像。 根据权利要求7所述的装置,其中,所述backbone网络的前2层采用res结构,网络的后2层采用inception结构。 根据权利要求7所述的装置,其中,所述第四装置,还用于识别所述瑕疵检测结果的置信度是否大于第一预设阈值,若大于所述第一预设阈值,则输出包括电子设备的边框的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在电子设备的边框中的位置的结果信息。 根据权利要求7所述的装置,其中,还包括第五装置,包括: 第五一装置,用于预设FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型及其初始的模型参数; 第五二装置,用于将样本电子设备的边框外观区域图像输入带有当前的模型参数的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型,得到样本电子设备的边框的瑕疵预测结果,所述瑕疵预测结果包括:样本电子设备的边框的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在样本电子设备的边框中的位置和瑕疵检测结果的置信度; 第五三装置,用于基于预设目标函数计算所述瑕疵预测结果与样本电子设备的真实瑕疵结果之间的差值,识别所述差值是否大于第二预设阈,若所述差值大于第二预设阈值,则执行第五四装置,用于基于所述差值更新所述FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型参数后,重新从第五二装置开始执行; 若所述差值小于等于第二预设阈值,则执行第五五装置,将带有当前 的模型参数的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型作为训练结束后的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型。 根据权利要求7所述的装置,其中,所述第二装置,用于将所述边框外观区域图像的长度方向进行缩放,并将所述边框外观区域图像的宽度方向进行填充。 一种基于计算的设备,其中,包括: 处理器;以及被安排成存储计算机可执行指令的存储器,所述可执行指令在被执行时使所述处理器: 获取电子设备的外观图像; 从所述电子设备的外观图像中提取该电子设备的边框外观区域图像,将所述边框外观区域图像调整为长度和宽度相同的图像; 将调整后的边框外观区域图像输入训练结束后的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型; 从所述FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型接收输出的电子设备的边框外观区域的瑕疵检测结果,所述瑕疵检测结果包括:电子设备的边框的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在电子设备的边框中的位置和瑕疵检测结果的置信度。 一种计算机可读存储介质,其上存储有计算机可执行指令,其中,该计算机可执行指令被处理器执行时使得该处理器: 获取电子设备的外观图像; 从所述电子设备的外观图像中提取该电子设备的边框外观区域图像,将所述边框外观区域图像调整为长度和宽度相同的图像; 将调整后的边框外观区域图像输入训练结束后的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型; 从所述FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型接收输出的电子设备的边框外观区域的瑕疵检测结果,所述瑕疵检测结果包括:电子设备的边框的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在电子设备的边框中的位置和瑕疵检测结果的置信度。

A method for detecting the appearance defect of the frame of an electronic device, wherein the method includes: Obtain the appearance image of the electronic device; Extract the frame appearance area image of the electronic device from the appearance image of the electronic device, and adjust the frame appearance area image to an image with the same length and width; Input the adjusted frame appearance area image into the FPN network after training and the model of the backbone network; The defect detection result of the frame appearance area of the electronic device received and output from the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network, the defect detection result includes: the defect type of the frame of the electronic device, the position of the defect in the frame of the electronic device, and the defect The confidence level of the test result. The method according to claim 1, wherein extracting the frame appearance area image of the electronic device from the appearance image of the electronic device comprises: The Unet instance segmentation method is used to extract the frame appearance area image of the electronic device from the appearance image of the electronic device. The method according to claim 1, wherein the first two layers of the backbone network adopt a res structure, and the last two layers of the network adopt an inception structure. The method according to claim 1, wherein after receiving the output of the defect detection result of the frame appearance area of the electronic device from the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network, the method further comprises: Identify whether the confidence level of the defect detection result is greater than a first preset threshold, and if it is greater than the first preset threshold, output result information including the defect type of the frame of the electronic device and the position of the defect in the frame of the electronic device . The method according to claim 1, wherein before inputting the frame appearance area image into the FPN network combined with the backbone network model, the method further comprises: Step 1, preset the FPN network combined with the backbone network model and its initial model parameters; Step 2: Input the frame appearance area image of the sample electronic device into the FPN network with the current model parameters combined with the backbone network model to obtain the defect prediction result of the frame of the sample electronic device. The defect prediction result includes: The type of border defect, the position of the defect in the border of the sample electronic device, and the confidence level of the defect detection result; Step 3: Calculate the difference between the defect prediction result and the real defect result of the sample electronic device based on the preset objective function, identify whether the difference is greater than a second preset threshold, and if the difference is greater than the second preset Set the threshold, then step four, based on the difference value to update the model parameters of the FPN network combined with the backbone network, restart from step two; If the difference is less than or equal to the second preset threshold, step 5, the FPN network combined with the backbone network model with the current model parameters is used as the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network after the training. The method according to claim 1, wherein adjusting the frame appearance area image to an image with the same length and width comprises: The length direction of the frame appearance area image is scaled, and the width direction of the frame appearance area image is filled. An electronic device frame appearance defect detection device, wherein the device includes: The first device is used to obtain the appearance image of the electronic device; The second device is used to extract the frame appearance area image of the electronic device from the appearance image of the electronic device, and adjust the frame appearance area image to an image with the same length and width; The third device is used to input the adjusted frame appearance area image into the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network after the training; The fourth device is used to receive and output the defect detection result of the frame appearance area of the electronic device from the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network, and the defect detection result includes: the defect type of the frame of the electronic device, and the defect in the electronic device The position in the frame and the confidence level of the defect detection result. 7. The device according to claim 7, wherein the second device is configured to extract the frame appearance area image of the electronic device from the appearance image of the electronic device by using Unet instance segmentation. The device according to claim 7, wherein the first two layers of the backbone network adopt a res structure, and the last two layers of the network adopt an inception structure. The device according to claim 7, wherein the fourth device is also used to identify whether the confidence level of the defect detection result is greater than a first preset threshold, and if it is greater than the first preset threshold, the output includes The result information of the defect type of the frame of the electronic device and the position of the defect in the frame of the electronic device. The device according to claim 7, further comprising a fifth device, including: The fifth device is used to preset the FPN network combined with the backbone network model and its initial model parameters; The fifth and second device is used to input the frame appearance area image of the sample electronic device into the FPN network with current model parameters combined with the backbone network model to obtain the defect prediction result of the frame of the sample electronic device, and the defect prediction result includes: The type of defect in the frame of the sample electronic device, the position of the defect in the frame of the sample electronic device, and the confidence level of the defect detection result; The fifty-third device is used to calculate the difference between the defect prediction result and the real defect result of the sample electronic device based on a preset objective function, and to identify whether the difference is greater than a second preset threshold, and if the difference is If it is greater than the second preset threshold, execute the fifth and fourth device for updating the model parameters of the FPN network combined with the backbone network based on the difference, and then restart execution from the fifth and second device; If the difference is less than or equal to the second preset threshold, the fifth and fifth device is executed, and the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network with the current model parameters is used as the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network after the training. 7. The device according to claim 7, wherein the second device is configured to scale the length direction of the frame appearance area image and fill the width direction of the frame appearance area image. A computing-based device that includes: A processor; and a memory arranged to store computer-executable instructions that, when executed, cause the processor to: Obtain the appearance image of the electronic device; Extract the frame appearance area image of the electronic device from the appearance image of the electronic device, and adjust the frame appearance area image to an image with the same length and width; Input the adjusted frame appearance area image into the FPN network after training and the model of the backbone network; The defect detection result of the frame appearance area of the electronic device received and output from the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network, the defect detection result includes: the defect type of the frame of the electronic device, the position of the defect in the frame of the electronic device, and the defect The confidence level of the test result. A computer-readable storage medium having computer-executable instructions stored thereon, wherein, when the computer-executable instructions are executed by a processor, the processor: Obtain the appearance image of the electronic device; Extract the frame appearance area image of the electronic device from the appearance image of the electronic device, and adjust the frame appearance area image to an image with the same length and width; Input the adjusted frame appearance area image into the FPN network after training and the model of the backbone network; The defect detection result of the frame appearance area of the electronic device received and output from the model of the FPN network combined with the backbone network, the defect detection result includes: the defect type of the frame of the electronic device, the position of the defect in the frame of the electronic device, and the defect The confidence level of the test result.

title: BACK COVER APPEARANCE DEFECT DETECTION METHOD FOR ELECTRONIC APPARATUS, AND APPARATUS; 电子设备背板外观瑕疵检测方法及设备; PROCÉDÉ DE DÉTECTION DE DÉFAUT D'ASPECT DE COUVERCLE ARRIÈRE POUR APPAREIL ÉLECTRONIQUE, ET APPAREIL
abstract:
Provided are a back cover appearance defect detection method for an electronic apparatus and an apparatus. The invention comprises: acquiring an appearance image of an electronic apparatus; extracting a back cover appearance region image of the electronic apparatus from the appearance image thereof, and adjusting the back cover appearance region image to be an image having an equal length and width; inputting the adjusted back cover appearance region image into a trained model combining an FPN network and a backbone network; and receiving a defect detection result of a back cover appearance region of the electronic apparatus from the model combining the FPN network and the backbone network, wherein the defect detection result comprises a defect type of a back cover of the electronic apparatus, a position of a defect in the back cover of the electronic apparatus, and a confidence level of the defect detection result. The invention accurately identifies appearance defect differences of electronic apparatus back covers of second-handed electronic apparatuses, such as mobile phones.

L'invention concerne un procédé de détection de défaut d'aspect de couvercle arrière pour un appareil électronique, ainsi qu'un appareil. L'invention consiste à : acquérir une image d'aspect d'un appareil électronique ; extraire une image de zone d'aspect de couvercle arrière de l'appareil électronique à partir de l'image d'aspect de celui-ci, puis ajuster l'image de zone d'aspect du couvercle arrière de façon à obtenir une image ayant une longueur et une largeur égales ; entrer l'image de zone d'aspect de couvercle arrière ajustée dans un modèle appris combinant un réseau FPN et un réseau fédérateur ; et recevoir un résultat de détection de défaut d'une zone d'aspect de couvercle arrière de l'appareil électronique en provenance du modèle combinant le réseau FPN et le réseau fédérateur, le résultat de détection de défaut comprenant un type de défaut d'un couvercle arrière de l'appareil électronique, une position d'un défaut dans le couvercle arrière de l'appareil électronique, ainsi qu'un niveau de confiance du résultat de détection de défaut. L'invention permet d'identifier précisément les différences de défauts d'aspect des couvercles arrière d'appareils électroniques de seconde main, tels que des téléphones mobiles.

步骤二,将样本电子设备的背板外观区域图像输入带有当前的模型参数的FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型,得到样本电子设备的背板的瑕疵预测结果,所述瑕疵预测结果包括:样本电子设备的背板的瑕疵种类、瑕疵在样本电子设备的背板中的位置和瑕疵检测结果的置信度; 步骤三,基于预设目标函数计算所述瑕疵预测结果与样本电子设备的真实瑕疵结果之间的差值,识别所述差值是否大于第二预设阈,若所述差值大于第二预设阈值,则步骤四,基于所述差值更新所述FPN网络结合backbone网络的模型参数后,重新从步骤二开始执行; title: INFRARED THERMAL IMAGING DETECTION DEVICE AND DETECTION METHOD FOR ELECTRONIC COMPONENT DEFECTS; 用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置及检测方法
abstract:
本发明涉及一种用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置及检测方法，包括依次设置在传送带上方的激光加热器和红外图像传感器及光学模组，激光加热器对传送带上的电子元器件加热，红外图像传感器及光学模组对加热多的电子元器件进行拍摄。本发明通过红外热成像，不仅可以获得划痕、崩角、裂痕等缺陷特征，还能捕获暗裂特征，并通过神经网络对图像缺陷进行目标检测，大大提高了检测效率。采用的红外热成像图片，相较于可见光图像，由更少的噪点、灰尘、污渍干扰，可以一定程度压制表层丝印纹理，避免噪点和丝印对缺陷检测的干扰。

The present invention relates to an infrared thermal imaging detection device and method for electronic component defects, comprising a laser heater, an infrared image sensor, and an optical module sequentially arranged above a conveyor belt, wherein the laser heater heats the electronic components on the conveyor belt, and the infrared image sensor and the optical module photograph the electronic components that are heated more. The present invention can not only obtain defect features such as scratches, chipped corners, and cracks through infrared thermal imaging, but also capture dark crack features, and perform target detection of image defects through neural networks, thereby greatly improving detection efficiency. Compared with visible light images, the infrared thermal imaging images used are less interfered by noise, dust, and stains, and can suppress the surface silk-screen texture to a certain extent, avoiding the interference of noise and silk-screen on defect detection.
claims:
1 .用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法,所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置，其特征在于，包括依次设置在传送带⑷上方的激光加热器⑵ 和红外图像传感器及光学模组⑴，激光加热器⑵对传送带⑷上的电子元器件⑶加热， 红外图像传感器及光学模组⑴对加热多的电子元器件⑶进行拍摄； 其特征在于，所述的检测方法包括以下步骤： 电子元器件⑶在传送带⑷上被激光加热器⑵加热，加热后按速度V到达红外图像传感器及光学模组⑴下方，红外图像传感器按时间间隔T进行拍摄，拍摄区间为s,元器件尺寸为u, T小于（s-2u）/v； 红外图像传感器周期性采集的图片中，电子元器件边缘在激光加热由于高度变化大， 温度较高，根据红外温度响应特征，边缘部分明显区分与其他区域，使用。2^y算子提取电子元器件轮廓，对轮廓取矩形包络分割，得到n个一次子图； 通过并行处理快速的对一帧图片中的电子元器件进行缺陷分析。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法，其特征在于，所述一次子图的并行处理方法包括以下步骤： S1、n个一次子图尺寸存在差异，需要做一定缩放，将子图大小转为w*h; S2、通过中值滤波去除椒盐噪声； S3、通过高通滤波器，突出边缘和缺陷图案； S4、对『NET的通道数缩小4倍，将把模型参数量缩小至2MB以内，得到轻量的『NET网络； S5、将预处理后的图片送入轻量的U-NET网络； S6、将U-NET分割的子图做矩形包络分割，得到二次子图； S7、将二次子图需要做一定缩放，得到大小为k*j二次子图； S8、将二次子图送入CNN分类网络，对区域进行分类； S9、整理所有二次的分类结果，判断一次子图中是否有分类为缺陷的二次子图;若没有，则该一次子图对应的电子元器件无缺陷，否则，列出所有二次子图的缺陷列表。 3.根据权利要求2所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法，其特征在于，所述U-NET网络的构建方法为： ⑴采集各类缺陷和正常工件红外热成像图片各1000张； ⑵采用人工标注缺陷位置和大小及缺陷类别； ⑶通过数据增强方法，增加各类样本数量至2000张以上； ⑷校验各类样本数量，通过3中的增强方法，保障各类别图像数量接近； ⑸对所有图片缩放，将子图大小转为w*h; （6）通过中值滤波去除椒盐噪声； ⑺通过高通滤波器，突出边缘和缺陷图案； ⑻将预处理后的图片作为训练集和验证集训练轻量的U-NET网络； （9）训练完成后保存训练模型； （10）加载训练模型，用于装置的缺陷检测。 4 .根据权利要求3所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法，其特征在于，所述缺陷的类别包含划痕、崩角、裂痕、暗裂、丝印、正常、其他七大类。 5 .根据权利要求3所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法，其特征在于，所述的数据增强方法包括旋转、缩放、灰度调整、增加噪声、随机裁剪。

1 .Detection method of infrared thermal imaging detection device for defects of electronic components, the infrared thermal imaging detection device for defects of electronic components, characterized in that it includes a laser heater (2) and an infrared image sensor and an optical module (1) which are sequentially arranged above a conveyor belt (4), the laser heater (2) heats the electronic components (3) on the conveyor belt (4), and the infrared image sensor and the optical module (1) take pictures of the electronic components (3) that are heated more; characterized in that the detection method includes the following steps: the electronic components (3) are heated by the laser heater (2) on the conveyor belt (4), and after heating, they reach the bottom of the infrared image sensor and the optical module (1) at a speed V, the infrared image sensor takes pictures at a time interval T, the shooting interval is s, the component size is u, and T is less than (s-2u)/v; in the pictures periodically collected by the infrared image sensor, the edge of the electronic component has a large height change during laser heating and a higher temperature. According to the infrared temperature response characteristics, the edge part is clearly distinguished from other areas and is used. The 2^y operator extracts the outline of the electronic component, and the outline is segmented by a rectangular envelope to obtain n primary sub-images; through parallel processing, the defect analysis of the electronic components in a frame of the image is quickly performed. 2 . The detection method of the infrared thermal imaging detection device for electronic component defects according to claim 1 is characterized in that the parallel processing method of the primary sub-image comprises the following steps: S1. The sizes of n primary sub-images are different, so some scaling is needed to convert the sub-image size to w*h; S2, remove salt and pepper noise by median filtering; S3, highlighting edges and defect patterns through a high-pass filter; S4. Reduce the number of NET channels by 4 times, reduce the model parameters to less than 2MB, and obtain a lightweight NET network; S5, send the preprocessed image to the lightweight U-NET network; S6, perform rectangular envelope segmentation on the sub-graph segmented by U-NET to obtain a secondary sub-graph; S7, scaling the secondary subgraph to obtain a secondary subgraph of size k*j; S8, sending the secondary sub-image into the CNN classification network to classify the region; S9. Arrange all secondary classification results and determine whether there is a secondary sub-graph classified as defective in the primary sub-graph; if not, the electronic components corresponding to the primary sub-graph are not defective, otherwise, list the defect lists of all secondary sub-graphs. 3. According to the detection method of the infrared thermal imaging detection device for electronic component defects in claim 2, it is characterized in that the construction method of the U-NET network is: (1) Collect 1000 infrared thermal imaging pictures of each type of defects and normal workpieces; (2) Manually mark the defect position, size and defect category; (3) Increase the number of samples of each type to more than 2000 by data enhancement method; (4) Verify the number of samples of each type, and ensure that the number of images of each category is close by the enhancement method in 3; (5) Scale all pictures and convert the sub-image size to w*h; (6) Remove salt and pepper noise through median filtering; (7) Highlight edges and defect patterns through high-pass filtering; (8) Use the preprocessed images as training sets and validation sets to train a lightweight U-NET network; (9) Save the training model after training; (10) Load the training model for defect detection of the device. 4 .The detection method of the infrared thermal imaging detection device for electronic component defects according to claim 3 is characterized in that the categories of defects include scratches, chipped corners, cracks, dark cracks, silk screen, normal, and other seven categories. 5 .The detection method of the infrared thermal imaging detection device for electronic component defects according to claim 3 is characterized in that the data enhancement method includes rotation, scaling, grayscale adjustment, noise addition, and random cropping.

title: INFRARED THERMAL IMAGING DETECTION DEVICE AND DETECTION METHOD FOR DEFECTS OF ELECTRONIC COMPONENTS; 用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置及检测方法
abstract:
本发明涉及一种用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置及检测方法，包括依次设置在传送带上方的激光加热器和红外图像传感器及光学模组，激光加热器对传送带上的电子元器件加热，红外图像传感器及光学模组对加热多的电子元器件进行拍摄。本发明通过红外热成像，不仅可以获得划痕、崩角、裂痕等缺陷特征，还能捕获暗裂特征，并通过神经网络对图像缺陷进行目标检测，大大提高了检测效率。采用的红外热成像图片，相较于可见光图像，由更少的噪点、灰尘、污渍干扰，可以一定程度压制表层丝印纹理，避免噪点和丝印对缺陷检测的干扰。

The invention relates to an infrared thermal imaging detection device and a detection method for defects of electronic components, including a laser heater, an infrared image sensor and an optical module which are sequentially arranged above a conveyor belt, and the laser heater is opposed to electronic components on the conveyor belt. Heating, infrared image sensor and optical module take pictures of electronic components that are heated more. Through infrared thermal imaging, the present invention can not only obtain defect features such as scratches, chipped corners, cracks, etc., but also capture dark crack features, and perform target detection on image defects through a neural network, which greatly improves the detection efficiency. Compared with visible light images, the infrared thermal imaging pictures used are less disturbed by noise, dust, and stains, and can suppress the surface silk screen texture to a certain extent, avoiding the interference of noise and silk screen on defect detection.
claims:
1 .用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置，其特征在于，包括依次设置在传送带 (4)上方的激光加热器⑵和红外图像传感器及光学模组⑴，激光加热器⑵对传送带(4) 上的电子元器件⑶加热，红外图像传感器及光学模组⑴对加热多的电子元器件⑶进行 拍摄。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法，其 特征在于，包括以下步骤： 电子元器件⑶在传送带⑷上被激光加热器⑵加热，加热后按速度v到达红外图像传 感器及光学模组(1)下方，红外图像传感器按时间间隔T进行拍摄，拍摄区间为s,元器件尺 寸为u,T小于(s-2u)/v。； 红外图像传感器周期性采集的图片中，电子元器件边缘在激光加热由于高度变化大， 温度较高，根据红外温度响应特征，边缘部分明显区分与其他区域，使用canny算子提取电 子元器件轮廓，对轮廓取矩形包络分割，得到n个一次子图； 通过并行处理快速的对一帧图片中的电子元器件进行缺陷分析。 3.根据权利要求2所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法，其 特征在于，所述的一次子图处理方法包括以下步骤： S1、n个一次子图尺寸存在差异，需要做一定缩放，将子图大小转为w*h； S2、通过中值滤波去除椒盐噪声； S3、通过高通滤波器，突出边缘和缺陷图案； S4、对U-NET的通道数缩小4倍，将把模型参数量缩小至2MB以内，得到轻量的U-NET网 络； S5、将预处理后的图片送入轻量的U-NET网络； S6、将U-NET分割的子图做矩形包络分割，得到二次子图； S7、将二次子图需要做一定缩放，得到大小为k*j二次子图； S8、将二次子图送入CNN分类网络，对区域进行分类； S9、整理所有二次的分类结果，判断一次子图中是否有分类为缺陷的二次子图；若没 有，则该一次子图对应的电子元器件无缺陷，否则，列出所有二次子图的缺陷列表。 4 .根据权利要求2所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法，其 特征在于，所述的U-NET网络构建的方法为： ⑴采集各类缺陷和正常工件红外热成像图片各1000张； ⑵采用人工标注缺陷位置和大小及缺陷类别； ⑶通过数据增强方法，增加各类样本数量至2000张以上； (4)校验各类样本数量，通过3中的增强方法，保障各类别图像数量接近； ⑸对所有图片缩放，将子图大小转为w*h ； (6)通过中值滤波去除椒盐噪声； ⑺通过高通滤波器，突出边缘和缺陷图案； (8)将预处理后的图片作为训练集和验证集训练轻量的U-NET网络； (9)训练完成后保存训练模型； (10)加载训练模型，用于装置的缺陷检测。 5 .根据权利要求2所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法，其 特征在于，所述的缺陷类别包含划痕、崩角、裂痕、暗裂、丝印、正常、其他七大类。 6 .根据权利要求2所述的用于电子元器件缺陷的红外热成像检测装置的检测方法，其 特征在于，所述的数据增强方法包括旋转、缩放、灰度调整、增加噪声、随机裁剪。

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR DEFECT DETECTION OF SEMI-SUPERVISED INDUSTRIAL PRODUCTS BASED ON POSITIVE SAMPLE LEARNING; 基于正样本学习的半监督工业制品瑕疵检测方法及系统
abstract:
一种基于正样本学习的半监督工业制品瑕疵检测方法及系统，通过正样本图像训练图像修复网络和瑕疵分割预测网络；采用含待测瑕疵的图像输入图像修复网络得到恢复后的正品图像，计算两者差值的绝对值后将三幅图像拼接得到检测张量，通过分割预测网络根据检测张量生成分割掩模二值图像，得到瑕疵区域。本发明使用半监督的深度学习方法，不需要提前标注像素级别的瑕疵位置数据，解决了瑕疵分割中的标注难题，只需要使用生产中的部分正品图像即可实现全自动化的训练学习，进行瑕疵分割和有无瑕疵的分类。并且能够对各类型异常瑕疵位置的像素进行分割。

A semi-supervised industrial product defect detection method and system based on positive sample learning, an image inpainting network and a defect segmentation prediction network are trained through positive sample images; an image containing defects to be tested is input to the image inpainting network to obtain a restored authentic image, and the calculation After the absolute value of the difference between the two, the three images are spliced to obtain the detection tensor, and the segmentation prediction network is used to generate the segmentation mask binary image according to the detection tensor to obtain the defect area. The invention uses the semi-supervised deep learning method, does not need to label the defect position data at the pixel level in advance, solves the problem of labeling in the defect segmentation, and only needs to use some genuine images in production to realize fully automated training and learning, and perform defect detection. Segmentation and flawless classification. And it can segment the pixels of various types of abnormal defect positions.

title: ROAD CRACK DETECTION METHOD AND SYSTEM BASED ON U-SHAPED CODEC NEURAL NETWORK; 一种基于U型编码解码器神经网络的道路裂缝检测方法及系统
abstract:
本发明公开了一种基于U型编码解码器神经网络的道路裂缝检测方法及系统，所述检测方法包括：获取初始道路裂缝数据集，对初始道路裂缝数据集扩展处理，得到第一道路裂缝数据集；构建U型编码解码器神经网络，U型编码解码器神经网络包括1组注意力模型层数加深模块；使用第二道路裂缝数据集验证所述训练好的U型编码解码器神经网络，得到验证通过后的最终U型编码解码器神经网络；将最终U型编码解码器神经网络嵌入到移动设备，进行道路裂缝的实际检测。在U‑Net网络的编解码器神经网络基础上加入注意力模型层数加深模块，用于道路裂缝的检测，减少了模型参数，进而减少模型处理图像的时间，便于嵌入到移动设备进行实时道路裂缝检测。

The invention discloses a road crack detection method and system based on a U-shaped codec neural network. The detection method includes: acquiring an initial road crack data set, and expanding and processing the initial road crack data set to obtain first road crack data Set; construct a U-shaped codec neural network, the U-shaped codec neural network includes a group of attention model layer number deepening module; use the second road crack data set to verify the trained U-shaped codec neural network, The final U-shaped codec neural network is obtained after the verification is passed; the final U-shaped codec neural network is embedded in the mobile device to perform the actual detection of road cracks. On the basis of the codec neural network of the UNet network, the attention model layer number deepening module is added to detect road cracks, reducing model parameters, thereby reducing the time for the model to process images, and facilitating embedding into mobile devices for real-time roads Crack detection.

title: A VISION-BASED METHOD FOR DETECTING DEFORMATION OF CONTAINER DOOR LOCK LEVER; 一种基于视觉的集装箱门锁杆变形检测方法
abstract:
本发明公开了一种基于视觉的集装箱门锁杆变形检测方法，包括如下步骤：采集图像并标注；训练检测模型和分割模型；获取当前作业集装箱的箱门图像；图像检测裁剪；利用集装箱门锁杆分割模型对裁剪后的图像进行前景分割；对门锁杆二值图像进行连通域标记，然后对每个连通域分别提取骨架；对所有点的二次导数值的绝对值求和；特征值大于阈值，则判断该连通域对应的门锁杆存在变形，反之则该连通域对应的门锁杆正常。本发明利用视觉检测技术在集装箱装卸作业过程中自动对其门锁杆进行检测并返回结果。解决了现有基于人工的集装箱门锁杆检测的缺点且现有常规的视觉检测算法无法直接应用于门锁杆检测的问题。

The invention discloses a vision-based method for detecting the deformation of a container door lock lever, which includes the following steps: collecting images and marking them; training detection models and segmentation models; acquiring the image of the current container door; image detection and cropping; using the container door lock The rod segmentation model performs foreground segmentation on the cropped image; performs connected domain labeling on the binary image of the door lock lever, and then extracts the skeleton for each connected domain; sums the absolute value of the second derivative value of all points; the feature value is greater than Threshold, it is judged that the door lock lever corresponding to the connected domain is deformed, otherwise, the door lock lever corresponding to the connected domain is normal. The invention uses the visual inspection technology to automatically detect the door lock lever of the container during the loading and unloading operation of the container and returns the result. It solves the shortcomings of the existing manual-based container door lock rod detection and the existing conventional visual inspection algorithm cannot be directly applied to the door lock rod detection problem.

title: A VISION-BASED METHOD FOR DETECTING DEFORMATION OF CONTAINER DOOR LOCK LEVER; 一种基于视觉的集装箱门锁杆变形检测方法
abstract:
本发明公开了一种基于视觉的集装箱门锁杆变形检测方法，包括如下步骤：采集图像并标注；训练检测模型和分割模型；获取当前作业集装箱的箱门图像；图像检测裁剪；利用集装箱门锁杆分割模型对裁剪后的图像进行前景分割；对门锁杆二值图像进行连通域标记，然后对每个连通域分别提取骨架；对所有点的二次导数值的绝对值求和；特征值大于阈值，则判断该连通域对应的门锁杆存在变形，反之则该连通域对应的门锁杆正常。本发明利用视觉检测技术在集装箱装卸作业过程中自动对其门锁杆进行检测并返回结果。解决了现有基于人工的集装箱门锁杆检测的缺点且现有常规的视觉检测算法无法直接应用于门锁杆检测的问题。

The invention discloses a vision-based method for detecting the deformation of a container door lock lever, which includes the following steps: collecting images and marking them; training detection models and segmentation models; acquiring the image of the current container door; image detection and cropping; using the container door lock The rod segmentation model performs foreground segmentation on the cropped image; performs connected domain labeling on the binary image of the door lock lever, and then extracts the skeleton for each connected domain; sums the absolute value of the second derivative value of all points; the feature value is greater than Threshold, it is determined that the door lock lever corresponding to the connected domain is deformed, otherwise, the door lock lever corresponding to the connected domain is normal. The invention utilizes the visual detection technology to automatically detect the door lock lever of the container during the loading and unloading operation of the container and return the result. It solves the shortcomings of the existing manual-based container door lock rod detection and the problem that the existing conventional visual inspection algorithms cannot be directly applied to the door lock rod detection.

title: METHOD FOR DETECTING AND RECOGNIZING SURFACE DEFECTS OF COMPOSITE WIRE LAYING BASED ON POINT CLOUD TO IMAGE; 一种基于点云转图像的复材铺丝表面缺陷检测与识别方法
abstract:
本发明涉及一种基于点云转图像的复材铺丝表面缺陷检测与识别方法，包括：获取复合材料铺丝产品的表面点云数据；对表面点云数据进行平面拟合，得到拟合平面；计算表面点云数据中所有点到拟合平面的距离；将表面点云数据进行OBB外包围盒包络，并与步骤S3计算的距离数据结合，生成灰度图；构建预训练的铺丝缺陷类型语义分割网络，将生成的灰度图作为输入，对灰度图进行缺陷区域分割与识别；将铺丝缺陷类型语义分割网络输出的分割结果映射至点云，并进行缺陷评估与可视化。本发明可实现复材铺丝表面缺陷的高精度测量与缺陷类型识别，解决现有技术中复材铺丝表面缺陷检测与识别时检测效率和精度低、适用性差等问题。

The invention relates to a method for detecting and recognizing surface defects of composite wire laying based on point cloud conversion image, including: obtaining surface point cloud data of composite wire laying products; performing plane fitting on the surface point cloud data to obtain a fitting plane ; Calculate the distance from all points in the surface point cloud data to the fitting plane; Envelope the surface point cloud data with the OBB outer bounding box, and combine it with the distance data calculated in step S3 to generate a grayscale image; construct a pre-trained silk laying The defect type semantic segmentation network takes the generated grayscale image as input, and performs defect area segmentation and recognition on the grayscale image; maps the segmentation results output by the silk-laying defect type semantic segmentation network to the point cloud, and performs defect evaluation and visualization. The invention can realize the high-precision measurement and defect type recognition of the surface defects of the composite wire laying, and solves the problems of low detection efficiency and accuracy, poor applicability and the like in the detection and recognition of the surface defects of the composite wire laying in the prior art.

title: METHOD FOR DETECTING AND RECOGNIZING SURFACE DEFECTS OF COMPOSITE WIRE LAYING BASED ON POINT CLOUD TO IMAGE; 一种基于点云转图像的复材铺丝表面缺陷检测与识别方法
abstract:
本发明涉及一种基于点云转图像的复材铺丝表面缺陷检测与识别方法，包括：获取复合材料铺丝产品的表面点云数据；对表面点云数据进行平面拟合，得到拟合平面；计算表面点云数据中所有点到拟合平面的距离；将表面点云数据进行OBB外包围盒包络，并与步骤S3计算的距离数据结合，生成灰度图；构建预训练的铺丝缺陷类型语义分割网络，将生成的灰度图作为输入，对灰度图进行缺陷区域分割与识别；将铺丝缺陷类型语义分割网络输出的分割结果映射至点云，并进行缺陷评估与可视化。本发明可实现复材铺丝表面缺陷的高精度测量与缺陷类型识别，解决现有技术中复材铺丝表面缺陷检测与识别时检测效率和精度低、适用性差等问题。

The invention relates to a method for detecting and recognizing surface defects of composite wire laying based on point cloud conversion image, including: obtaining surface point cloud data of composite wire laying products; performing plane fitting on the surface point cloud data to obtain a fitting plane ; Calculate the distance from all points in the surface point cloud data to the fitting plane; Envelope the surface point cloud data with an OBB outer bounding box, and combine it with the distance data calculated in step S3 to generate a grayscale image; construct a pre-trained silk laying The defect type semantic segmentation network takes the generated gray image as input, and performs defect area segmentation and recognition on the gray image; maps the segmentation results output by the wire laying defect type semantic segmentation network to the point cloud, and performs defect evaluation and visualization. The invention can realize the high-precision measurement and defect type recognition of the surface defects of the composite wire laying, and solves the problems of low detection efficiency and accuracy and poor applicability in the detection and recognition of the composite wire laying surface defects in the prior art.

title: A SEMANTIC SEGMENTATION METHOD FOR RAIL SURFACE DEFECTS; 一种铁轨表面缺陷语义分割方法
abstract:
本发明公开了一种铁轨表面缺陷语义分割方法，包括如下步骤：采集样本图像，制作相应样本集；建立卷积神经网络，所述卷积神经网络包括UNet++网络结构和ECA注意力模块；将建立好的样本集输入语义分割模型，对模型内的权重参数进行训练更新，直到模型的损失函数不再继续收敛，得到训练好的模型；将采集得到的铁轨表面缺陷图像输入训练好的模型，得到语义分割结果。本发明检测速度快，能够做到实时检测，同时有较高的检测精度，满足检测需求，也能够应用于恶劣环境下的生产任务。

The present invention discloses a semantic segmentation method for rail surface defects, which includes the following steps: collecting sample images and making corresponding sample sets; establishing a convolutional neural network, the convolutional neural network including UNet++ network structure and ECA attention module; The good sample set is input into the semantic segmentation model, and the weight parameters in the model are trained and updated until the loss function of the model no longer converges, and the trained model is obtained; the collected rail surface defect images are input into the trained model to obtain Semantic segmentation results. The invention has fast detection speed, can achieve real-time detection, has higher detection accuracy, meets detection requirements, and can also be applied to production tasks in harsh environments.
claims:
L 一种铁轨表面缺陷语义分割方法，其特征在于，包括如下步骤： SL采集样本图像,制作相应样本集； S2：建立卷积神经网络，所述卷积神经网络包括UNet++网络结构和ECA注意力模块，所 述UNet++网络结构用于下采样与上采样，所述ECA注意力模块用于信道交互； S3 ：将建立好的样本集输入卷积神经网络,对模型内的权重参数进行训练更新,直到模 型的损失函数不再继续收敛，得到训练好的模型； S4：将采集得到的铁轨表面缺陷图像输入步骤S3中训练好的模型,得到语义分割结果。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的一种铁轨表面缺陷语义分割方法，其特征在于，所述步骤S1中 样本集中包括对应标签，所述标签指代每个像素点的类别，缺陷目标与背景的像素点采用 不同标签。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的一种铁轨表面缺陷语义分割方法，其特征在于，所述步骤S2中 UNet++网络结构包括多个下采样层与上采样层。 4 .根据权利要求1所述的一种铁轨表面缺陷语义分割方法，其特征在于，所述步骤S2中 ECA注意力模块采用了不降维的局部跨信道交互方法，具体为:每个通道的注意力预测是通 过大小为k的快速一维卷积来实现。 5 .根据权利要求1所述的一种铁轨表面缺陷语义分割方法，其特征在于，所述步骤S3中 卷积神经网络对于输入样本集的处理具体为： A1 ：将样本图像输入网络模型进行编码与解码操作； A2：在卷积层中,对不同尺度的图像进行编码下采样，得到不同尺度的特征图； A3 ：在上采样层中,对步骤A2得到的特征图进行解码； A4：在ECA注意力模块中，对解码后的特征图进行信道交互，训练通道注意力。 6 .根据权利要求1所述的一种铁轨表面缺陷语义分割方法，其特征在于，所述步骤S3中 损失函数采用Log-Cosh Dice损失函数，具体为： A与B代表预测区域与真实区域,DSC代表区域间的Dice损失，则： DSC = 2 ΙΑ1 |A|+|B| cosh函数为： e* + e -x cosh(x)=——-—— 其中，ex和e-x代表与自然对数相关的指数函数； 则损失函数为： loss = log (cosh (DSC))。 7 .根据权利要求1所述的一种铁轨表面缺陷语义分割方法，其特征在于，所述步骤S4中 语义分割结果用二值化图像标记分割结果。

L A method for semantic segmentation of rail surface defects, which is characterized in that it comprises the following steps: SL collects sample images and makes corresponding sample sets; S2: Establish a convolutional neural network, the convolutional neural network including an UNet++ network structure and an ECA attention module, the UNet++ network structure is used for down-sampling and up-sampling, and the ECA attention module is used for channel interaction; S3: Input the established sample set into the convolutional neural network, and train and update the weight parameters in the model until the loss function of the model no longer continues to converge, and a trained model is obtained; S4: Input the acquired rail surface defect image into the model trained in step S3 to obtain the semantic segmentation result. 2 The method for semantic segmentation of rail surface defects according to claim 1, wherein the sample set in step S1 includes corresponding labels, and the labels refer to the category of each pixel, the defect target and the background pixel The points use different labels. 3 The method for semantic segmentation of rail surface defects according to claim 1, wherein the UNet++ network structure in step S2 includes multiple down-sampling layers and up-sampling layers. 4 A method for semantic segmentation of rail surface defects according to claim 1, wherein the ECA attention module in step S2 adopts a non-dimensional local cross-channel interaction method, specifically: attention of each channel Force prediction is achieved by fast one-dimensional convolution of size k. 5 A method for semantic segmentation of rail surface defects according to claim 1, wherein the processing of the input sample set by the convolutional neural network in the step S3 is specifically: A1: Input the sample image into the network model for encoding and decoding operations; A2: In the convolutional layer, images of different scales are coded and down-sampled to obtain feature maps of different scales; A3: In the up-sampling layer, decode the feature map obtained in step A2; A4: In the ECA attention module, channel interaction is performed on the decoded feature map to train channel attention. 6 The method for semantic segmentation of rail surface defects according to claim 1, wherein the loss function in step S3 adopts the Log-Cosh Dice loss function, which is specifically: A and B represent the predicted area and the real area, and DSC represents the Dice loss between the areas, then: DSC = 2 ΙΑ1 |A|+|B| The cosh function is: e* + e -x cosh(x)=- Among them, ex and ex represent exponential functions related to natural logarithms; then the loss function is: loss = log (cosh (DSC)). 7 The method for semantic segmentation of rail surface defects according to claim 1, wherein the semantic segmentation result in step S4 is marked with a binary image.

title: DEVICE FOR DETECTING SURFACE DEFECTS ON AT LEAST ONE END FACE OF AT LEAST ONE OPTICAL FIBER; 检测至少一个光纤的至少一个端面上的表面缺陷的装置
abstract:
本发明涉及一种检测光纤(14)的端面(16)上的表面缺陷(62‑65)的装置(10)，所述装置包括：‑数字显微镜(15)，该数字显微镜被配置为捕获端面(16)的图像(img1)；以及‑用于分析所述图像(img1)的装置(25)，该装置被配置为检测存在于端面(16)上的表面缺陷，该分析装置集成“U‑Net”型神经网络(26)，该“U‑Net”型神经网络使其训练阶段经由增强器执行；所述增强器被配置为基于参考图像创建旨在训练所述神经网络的训练图像；所述训练图像通过仅对所述参考图像施加翻转、旋转和/或亮度、对比度或阴影变化而从所述参考图像获得。

The invention relates to a device (10) for detecting surface defects (62-65) on the end face (16) of an optical fiber (14), the device comprising:-a digital microscope (15) configured to capture the end face The image (img1) of (16); and-a device (25) for analyzing the image (img1), the device is configured to detect surface defects existing on the end surface (16), and the analysis device is integrated with "U- Net" type neural network (26), the "U-Net" type neural network has its training phase performed via an enhancer; the enhancer is configured to create a training image aimed at training the neural network based on a reference image; so The training image is obtained from the reference image by applying only inversion, rotation, and/or brightness, contrast, or shading changes to the reference image.
claims:
1.一种检测至少一个光纤(14)的至少一个端面(16)上的表面缺陷(62-65)的装置 (10),所述装置包括： -数字显微镜(15)，所述数字显微镜(15)被配置为捕获所述至少一个端面(16)的图像 (img1)；以及 -用于分析所述图像(img1)的装置(25)，所述装置(25)被配置为检测存在于所述至少 一个端面(16)上的表面缺陷(62-65)； 其特征在于，所述分析装置(25)集成“U-Net”型神经网络(26)，所述“U-Net”型神经网 络(26)使其训练阶段经由增强器(52)执行;所述增强器(52)被配置为基于参考图像(iref) 创建旨在训练所述神经网络(26)的训练图像(iref,)；所述训练图像(iref,)通过仅对所述 参考图像(iref)施加翻转、旋转和/或亮度、对比度或阴影变化而从所述参考图像(iref)获 得。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的表面缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，一个或多个光纤(14)被聚集 在连接器(13)中，所述数字显微镜(15)被配置为在所述连接器(13)的水平上捕获所述光纤 (14)的所述端面(16)的图像;并且所述分析装置(25)被配置为隔离所述光纤(14)的所述端 面(16)。 3 .根据权利要求1或2所述的表面缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，所述“U-Net”型神经网络 (26)包括收缩路径(35)和扩张路径(36)，各个路径具有五个处理层(40a-40e、41a-41e)。 4 .根据权利要求3所述的表面缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，所述收缩路径(35)和所述扩 张路径(36)对于各个处理层(40a-40e、41a-41e)实施两个处理(42)，所述两个处理(42)执 行三个像素乘三个像素的卷积,随后是线性校正。 5 .根据权利要求3或4所述的表面缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，所述收缩路径(35)在各 个处理层(40a-40e)之间实施执行局部最大值的线性级联的处理(43)。 6 .根据权利要求3至5所述的表面缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，所述扩张路径(36)在各 个处理层(41a-41e)之间实施执行两个像素乘两个像素的向上卷积的处理(45)。 7 .根据权利要求1至6中任意一项所述的表面缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，所述参考图 像(iref)的数量在200至400的范围内，所述增强器(52)被配置为获得大于1000的数量的训 练图像(iref,)。 8 .根据权利要求1至7中任意一项所述的表面缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，所述增强器 (52)随机地选择向所述参考图像(iref)施加或不施加翻转、旋转和/或亮度、对比度或阴影 变化。 9 .根据权利要求1至8中任意一项所述的表面缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，所述增强器 (52)随机地选择施加到所述参考图像(iref)的翻转、旋转和/或亮度、对比度或阴影变化的 参数。

1. A device (10) for detecting surface defects (62-65) on at least one end face (16) of at least one optical fiber (14), the device comprising: -A digital microscope (15) configured to capture an image (img1) of the at least one end face (16); and -A device (25) for analyzing the image (img1), the device (25) is configured to detect surface defects (62-65) existing on the at least one end surface (16); characterized in that, The analysis device (25) integrates a "U-Net" type neural network (26), and the "U-Net" type neural network (26) has its training phase performed via an enhancer (52); the enhancer ( 52) is configured to create a training image (iref,) aimed at training the neural network (26) based on a reference image (iref); , Rotation and/or brightness, contrast or shadow changes are obtained from the reference image (iref). 2 The surface defect detection device according to claim 1, characterized in that one or more optical fibers (14) are gathered in a connector (13), and the digital microscope (15) is configured to be in the connector An image of the end face (16) of the optical fiber (14) is captured at the level of (13); and the analysis device (25) is configured to isolate the end face (16) of the optical fiber (14). 3 The surface defect detection device according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that the "U-Net" type neural network (26) includes a contraction path (35) and an expansion path (36), each path has five Treatment layer (40a-40e, 41a-41e). 4 The surface defect detection device according to claim 3, wherein the contraction path (35) and the expansion path (36) implement two treatments (40a-40e, 41a-41e) for each treatment layer (40a-40e, 41a-41e). 42), the two processes (42) perform a convolution of three pixels by three pixels, followed by linear correction. 5 The surface defect detection device according to claim 3 or 4, characterized in that the contraction path (35) implements a linear cascade of local maximum processing (43) between the respective processing layers (40a-40e). ). 6 The surface defect detection device according to claims 3 to 5, characterized in that the expansion path (36) implements an upward convolution of two pixels by two pixels between each processing layer (41a-41e)The treatment(45). 7 The surface defect detection device according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the number of the reference images (iref) is in the range of 200 to 400, and the enhancer (52) is configured to Obtain more than 1000 training images (iref,). 8 The surface defect detection device according to any one of claims 1 to 7, characterized in that the intensifier (52) randomly chooses to apply or not to apply inversion, rotation and/or to the reference image (iref) Or brightness, contrast, or shadow changes. 9 The surface defect detection device according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the enhancer (52) randomly selects the flip, rotation and/or brightness applied to the reference image (iref) , Contrast or shadow change parameters.

title: WELDING DEFECT DETECTION METHOD, DEVICE AND TERMINAL EQUIPMENT BASED ON IMPROVED U-NET; 基于改进U-NET的焊接缺陷检测方法、装置及终端设备
abstract:
本发明涉及一种基于改进U‑net的焊接缺陷检测方法、装置及终端设备，对训练样本集进行标注，得到标注数据，根据训练样本集以及标注数据对U型分割网络进行训练，其中，U型分割网络中的分割编码器和分割解码器之间使用跳跃连接，用于增强上下文语义信息，提高分割网络对微小焊接缺陷部位的特征提取的准确性，U型分割网络中引入全局注意力机制，产生更具分辨性的特征，使分割网络更好地聚焦于缺陷区域的特征表达，增强分割网络对焊接缺陷区域的特征表达能力，降低背景图像的干扰，从而提高网络的分割性能，最后将待检测焊接图像输入至训练好的语义分割模型中进行缺陷检测，能够提升焊接缺陷检测准确性。

The present invention relates to a welding defect detection method, device and terminal equipment based on the improved U-net. The training sample set is marked to obtain the marked data, and the U-shaped segmentation network is trained according to the training sample set and the marked data, wherein, U A skip connection is used between the segmentation encoder and the segmentation decoder in the U-shaped segmentation network to enhance the contextual semantic information and improve the accuracy of the feature extraction of the segmentation network for tiny welding defects. A global attention mechanism is introduced in the U-shaped segmentation network , produce more discriminative features, make the segmentation network better focus on the feature expression of the defect area, enhance the feature expression ability of the segmentation network to the welding defect area, reduce the interference of the background image, thereby improving the segmentation performance of the network, and finally The welding image to be detected is input into the trained semantic segmentation model for defect detection, which can improve the accuracy of welding defect detection.
claims:
1 .一种基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法，其特征在于，包括： 获取训练样本集，所述训练样本集包括至少两个焊接缺陷样本图像； 对所述训练样本集进行标注，得到标注数据； 将所述训练样本集输入至U型分割网络中的分割编码器中，所述分割编码器通过多层卷积层和池化层进行缺陷部位的特征提取，所述分割编码器输出给U型分割网络中的分割解码器中通过卷积层和上采样层进行上采样，输出与所述训练样本集相同大小的语义分割图；其中，所述U型分割网络中的分割编码器和分割解码器之间使用跳跃连接，且所述U型分割网络中引入全局注意力机制；将所述语义分割图与所述标注数据通过交叉熵损失函数进行运算，优化语义分割模型中的参数； 将待检测焊接图像输入至训练好的语义分割模型中进行缺陷检测； 所述分割编码器的每一层的输入设置为三部分的融合:所述分割编码器本层的上一层的输出、所述分割编码器本层的输出以及所述分割编码器本层的下一层的输出。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法，其特征在于，所述获取训练样本集之后，所述焊接缺陷检测方法还包括： 对所述训练样本集进行预处理。 3 .根据权利要求2所述的基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法，其特征在于，所述对所述训练样本集进行预处理，包括： 对所述训练样本集依次进行线性变换、Gamma变换、梯度直方图均衡化处理和图像大小转换。 4 .根据权利要求1所述的基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法，其特征在于，所述对所述训练样本集进行标注，得到标注数据，包括： 通过Labelme工具，对所述训练样本集进行标注，得到初步标注数据； 对所述初步标注数据进行二值化，得到二值化标注数据。 5 .一种基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，包括： 训练样本集获取模块，用于获取训练样本集，所述训练样本集包括至少两个焊接缺陷样本图像； 标注模块，用于对所述训练样本集进行标注，得到标注数据； 网络训练模块，用于将所述训练样本集输入至U型分割网络中的分割编码器中，所述分割编码器通过多层卷积层和池化层进行缺陷部位的特征提取，所述分割编码器输出给U型分割网络中的分割解码器中通过卷积层和上采样层进行上采样，输出与所述训练样本集相同大小的语义分割图；其中，所述U型分割网络中的分割编码器和分割解码器之间使用跳跃连接，且所述U型分割网络中引入全局注意力机制;将所述语义分割图与所述标注数据通过交叉熵损失函数进行运算，优化语义分割模型中的参数； 缺陷检测模块，用于将待检测焊接图像输入至训练好的语义分割模型中进行缺陷检测； 所述分割编码器的每一层的输入设置为三部分的融合:所述分割编码器本层的上一层的输出、所述分割编码器本层的输出以及所述分割编码器本层的下一层的输出。 6 .一种终端设备，包括存储器、处理器以及存储在所述存储器中并可在所述处理器上运行的计算机程序，其特征在于，所述处理器执行所述计算机程序时实现如权利要求1-4任 一项所述的基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法的步骤。

1 . A welding defect detection method based on improved U-net, characterized in that it comprises: obtaining a training sample set, the training sample set including at least two welding defect sample images; labeling the training sample set to obtain the label Data; The training sample set is input to the segmentation encoder in the U-shaped segmentation network, and the segmentation encoder performs feature extraction of defective parts through multi-layer convolutional layers and pooling layers, and the segmentation encoder is output to Upsampling is carried out by the convolutional layer and the upsampling layer in the segmentation decoder in the U-shaped segmentation network, and the semantic segmentation map of the same size as the training sample set is output; wherein, the segmentation encoder and the segmentation encoder in the U-shaped segmentation network Skip connections are used between the segmentation decoders, and a global attention mechanism is introduced into the U-shaped segmentation network; the semantic segmentation map and the labeled data are operated through a cross-entropy loss function to optimize parameters in the semantic segmentation model; The welding image to be detected is input into the trained semantic segmentation model for defect detection; the input of each layer of the segmentation coder is set to the fusion of three parts: the output of the upper layer of the current layer of the segmentation coder, The output of the current layer of the segmentation encoder and the output of the layer next to the current layer of the segmentation encoder. 2 . The improved U-net-based welding defect detection method according to claim 1, characterized in that, after the training sample set is acquired, the welding defect detection method further comprises: preprocessing the training sample set. 3 . the welding defect detection method based on improved U-net according to claim 2, is characterized in that, described training sample set is carried out preprocessing, comprises: Carrying out linear transformation, Gamma transformation to described training sample set successively , Gradient histogram equalization processing and image size conversion. 4 . the welding defect detection method based on improved U-net according to claim 1, is characterized in that, described training sample set is marked, obtains label data, comprises: By Labelme tool, described training sample set performing labeling to obtain preliminary labeling data; performing binarization on the preliminary labeling data to obtain binary labeling data. 5 . A welding defect detection device based on improved U-net, characterized in that it includes: a training sample set acquisition module, used to obtain a training sample set, the training sample set includes at least two welding defect sample images; a labeling module, It is used to mark the training sample set to obtain labeled data; the network training module is used to input the training sample set into the segmentation encoder in the U-shaped segmentation network, and the segmentation encoder passes multi-layer convolution Layer and pooling layer carry out feature extraction of defect parts, and the segmentation encoder outputs to the segmentation decoder in the U-shaped segmentation network to perform upsampling through the convolutional layer and the upsampling layer, and the output is the same size as the training sample set Semantic segmentation map; Wherein, skip connection is used between the segmentation encoder and the segmentation decoder in the U-shaped segmentation network, and a global attention mechanism is introduced in the U-shaped segmentation network; The semantic segmentation map is combined with the The above-mentioned labeled data is operated by a cross-entropy loss function to optimize the parameters in the semantic segmentation model; the defect detection module is used to input the welding image to be detected into the trained semantic segmentation model for defect detection; each of the segmentation encoders The input of one layer is set as the fusion of three parts: the output of the upper layer of the current layer of the segmentation encoder, the output of the current layer of the segmentation encoder and the output of the next layer of the current layer of the segmentation encoder. 6 . A terminal device, comprising a memory, a processor, and a computer program stored in the memory and operable on the processor, characterized in that, when the processor executes the computer program, the computer program according to claim 1 is realized. -4 appointments A step of the improved U-net-based welding defect detection method.

title: WELDING DEFECT DETECTION METHOD, DEVICE AND TERMINAL EQUIPMENT BASED ON IMPROVED U-NET; 基于改进U-NET的焊接缺陷检测方法、装置及终端设备
abstract:
本发明涉及一种基于改进U‑net的焊接缺陷检测方法、装置及终端设备，对训练样本集进行标注，得到标注数据，根据训练样本集以及标注数据对U型分割网络进行训练，其中，U型分割网络中的分割编码器和分割解码器之间使用跳跃连接，用于增强上下文语义信息，提高分割网络对微小焊接缺陷部位的特征提取的准确性，U型分割网络中引入全局注意力机制，产生更具分辨性的特征，使分割网络更好地聚焦于缺陷区域的特征表达，增强分割网络对焊接缺陷区域的特征表达能力，降低背景图像的干扰，从而提高网络的分割性能，最后将待检测焊接图像输入至训练好的语义分割模型中进行缺陷检测，能够提升焊接缺陷检测准确性。

The invention relates to a welding defect detection method, device and terminal equipment based on an improved U-net. The training sample set is marked to obtain marked data, and a U-shaped segmentation network is trained according to the training sample set and the marked data, where U A jump connection is used between the segmentation encoder and the segmentation decoder in the U-shaped segmentation network to enhance the contextual semantic information and improve the accuracy of the segmentation network for feature extraction of small welding defects. The U-shaped segmentation network introduces a global attention mechanism , To produce more distinguishable features, so that the segmentation network can better focus on the feature expression of the defect area, enhance the feature expression ability of the segmentation network to the welding defect area, reduce the interference of the background image, and improve the segmentation performance of the network. The welding image to be inspected is input into the trained semantic segmentation model for defect detection, which can improve the accuracy of welding defect detection.
claims:
1 .一种基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法，其特征在于，包括： 获取训练样本集，所述训练样本集包括至少两个焊接缺陷样本图像； 对所述训练样本集进行标注，得到标注数据； 将所述训练样本集输入至U型分割网络中的分割编码器中，所述分割编码器通过多层 卷积层和池化层进行缺陷部位的特征提取，所述分割编码器输出给U型分割网络中的分割 解码器中通过卷积层和上采样层进行上采样，输出与所述训练样本集相同大小的语义分割 图；其中，所述U型分割网络中的分割编码器和分割解码器之间使用跳跃连接，且所述U型分 割网络中引入全局注意力机制;将所述语义分割图与所述标注数据通过交叉熵损失函数进 行运算，优化语义分割模型中的参数； 将待检测焊接图像输入至训练好的语义分割模型中进行缺陷检测。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法，其特征在于，所述分割 编码器的每一层的输入设置为三部分的融合:所述分割编码器本层的上一层的输出、所述 分割编码器本层的输出以及所述分割编码器本层的下一层的输出。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法，其特征在于，所述获取 训练样本集之后，所述焊接缺陷检测方法还包括： 对所述训练样本集进行预处理。 4 .根据权利要求3所述的基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法，其特征在于，所述对所 述训练样本集进行预处理，包括： 对所述训练样本集依次进行线性变换、Gamma变换、梯度直方图均衡化处理和图像大小 转换。 5 .根据权利要求1所述的基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法，其特征在于，所述对所 述训练样本集进行标注，得到标注数据，包括： 通过Labelme工具，对所述训练样本集进行标注，得到初步标注数据； 对所述初步标注数据进行二值化，得到二值化标注数据。 6 .一种基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测装置，其特征在于，包括： 训练样本集获取模块，用于获取训练样本集，所述训练样本集包括至少两个焊接缺陷 样本图像； 标注模块，用于对所述训练样本集进行标注，得到标注数据； 网络训练模块，用于将所述训练样本集输入至U型分割网络中的分割编码器中，所述分 割编码器通过多层卷积层和池化层进行缺陷部位的特征提取，所述分割编码器输出给U型 分割网络中的分割解码器中通过卷积层和上采样层进行上采样，输出与所述训练样本集相 同大小的语义分割图；其中，所述U型分割网络中的分割编码器和分割解码器之间使用跳跃 连接，且所述U型分割网络中引入全局注意力机制;将所述语义分割图与所述标注数据通过 交叉熵损失函数进行运算，优化语义分割模型中的参数； 缺陷检测模块，用于将待检测焊接图像输入至训练好的语义分割模型中进行缺陷检 测。 7 .一种终端设备，包括存储器、处理器以及存储在所述存储器中并可在所述处理器上 运行的计算机程序，其特征在于，所述处理器执行所述计算机程序时实现如权利要求1 - 5任 一项所述的基于改进U-net的焊接缺陷检测方法的步骤。

title: A METHOD FOR OBTAINING COMPLETE SURFACE IMAGE OF BAR; 一种棒材完整表面图像获取方法
abstract:
本发明提供一种棒材完整表面图像获取方法，属于机器视觉检测技术领域。该方法通过四个线阵相机从不同角度采集棒材图像，通过分割算法得到棒材区域并进行骨架搜索；利用形变矫正算法将棒材区域变换到竖直状态并位于图像正中间，有效的避免了棒材生产过程中由于抖动造成的拍摄图像扭曲的问题，真实的体现了实际的棒材状态；依据相机和棒材的位置关系，计算出各相机像素坐标与实物坐标的转换关系，得到各相机拍摄图像的重合区域并进行剔除，保留下来的各相机图像横向进行拼接得到棒材的完整表面图像。棒材完整表面图像能更好的体现棒材的表面质量情况，同时也为后续的表面缺陷检测处理提供了较好的基础图像数据。

The invention provides a method for acquiring a complete surface image of a bar, which belongs to the technical field of machine vision detection. In this method, four line array cameras are used to collect bar images from different angles, and the bar area is obtained through a segmentation algorithm and skeleton search is performed; the bar area is transformed into a vertical state and located in the middle of the image by using a deformation correction algorithm, effectively avoiding The problem of image distortion caused by shaking in the bar production process is solved, and the actual state of the bar is truly reflected; according to the positional relationship between the camera and the bar, the conversion relationship between the pixel coordinates of each camera and the physical coordinates is calculated, and each The overlapping areas of the images captured by the camera are eliminated, and the remaining images of each camera are stitched horizontally to obtain a complete surface image of the bar. The complete surface image of the bar can better reflect the surface quality of the bar, and also provide better basic image data for subsequent surface defect detection processing.

本发明提供一种棒材完整表面图像获取方法，属于机器视觉检测技术领域。该方法通过四个线阵相机从不同角度采集棒材图像，通过分割算法得到棒材区域并进行骨架搜索；利用形变矫正算法将棒材区域变换到竖直状态并位于图像正中间，有效的避免了棒材生产过程中由于抖动造成的拍摄图像扭曲的问题，真实的体现了实际的棒材状态；依据相机和棒材的位置关系，计算出各相机像素坐标与实物坐标的转换关系，得到各相机拍摄图像的重合区域并进行剔除，保留下来的各相机图像横向进行拼接得到棒材的完整表面图像。棒材完整表面图像能更好的体现棒材的表面质量情况，同时也为后续的表面缺陷检测处理提供了较好的基础图像数据。

The invention provides a method for acquiring a complete surface image of a bar, which belongs to the technical field of machine vision detection. This method uses four line array cameras to collect bar images from different angles, obtains the bar area through a segmentation algorithm and performs a skeleton search; uses a deformation correction algorithm to transform the bar area to a vertical state and is located in the middle of the image, effectively avoiding The problem of image distortion caused by jitter in the bar production process is solved, which truly reflects the actual state of the bar; according to the positional relationship between the camera and the bar, the conversion relationship between the pixel coordinates of each camera and the physical coordinates is calculated, and each The overlapping area of the image taken by the camera is eliminated, and the remaining camera images are stitched horizontally to obtain a complete surface image of the bar. The complete surface image of the bar can better reflect the surface quality of the bar, and it also provides better basic image data for the subsequent surface defect detection and processing.

title: A PATCH-ORIENTED HIGH-RESOLUTION ANNUAL URBAN GREEN SPACE REMOTE SENSING INFORMATION EXTRACTION METHOD; 一种面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法
abstract:
本发明公开了一种面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法，包括以下步骤：获取某一年份的Google Earth高分辨率影像，根据所述Google Earth影像采集城市绿地样本；采用深度学习的方法对所述样本进行模型训练，应用训练好的模型对Google Earth影像进行分类处理，获得初始高分辨率绿地图斑；获取所述某一年份中逐月的多光谱影像；根据所有月份的多光谱影像计算月度归一化植被指数，获得年度多光谱绿地图斑；矢量化所述初始高分辨率绿地图斑和年度绿地图斑，然后对两种图斑进行空间关系分析；根据空间关系分析结果对所述初始高分辨率绿地图斑进行修正，获得修正后的年度绿地图斑。本发明能够实现精度稳定且高效的高分辨率的年度城市绿地信息的准确提取。

The invention discloses a pattern-oriented high-resolution annual urban green space remote sensing information extraction method, comprising the following steps: obtaining a Google Earth high-resolution image of a certain year, collecting urban green space samples according to the Google Earth image; The learning method performs model training on the sample, and applies the trained model to classify the Google Earth image to obtain an initial high-resolution green map spot; obtain the monthly multi-spectral image in a certain year; The monthly normalized vegetation index is calculated from the multispectral imagery of the multispectral image to obtain the annual multispectral green patch; the initial high-resolution green patch and the annual green patch are vectorized, and then the spatial relationship between the two patches is analyzed; The relationship analysis result corrects the initial high-resolution green patch to obtain the corrected annual green patch. The invention can realize accurate extraction of annual urban green space information with stable precision and high efficiency.
claims: 1 .一种面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法，其特征在于，包括以下 步骤： 获取某一年份的Google Earth高分辨率影像，根据所述Google Earth影像采集城市绿 地样本； 采用深度学习的方法对所述样本进行模型训练，应用训练好的模型对所述Google Earth影像进行分类处理，获得初始高分辨率绿地图斑； 获取所述某一年份中逐月的多光谱影像； 根据所有月份的多光谱影像计算月度归一化植被指数，获得年度多光谱绿地图斑； 矢量化所述初始高分辨率绿地图斑和所述年度绿地图斑，然后对所述初始高分辨率绿 地图斑和所述年度绿地图斑进行空间关系分析； 根据所述空间关系分析的结果对所述初始高分辨率绿地图斑进行修正，获得修正后的 年度绿地图斑； 根据所述空间关系分析的结果对所述初始高分辨率绿地图斑进行修正具体包括以下 三条并行的修正规则： 修正规则一：若Google Earth影像提取结果为面积大于阈值一的绿地，且所述多光谱 影像提取结果为非绿地，则去除所述绿地； 若Google Earth影像提取结果为绿地，且所述绿地的面积小于阈值一，则对所述绿地 进行目视检测；若是绿地则保留所述绿地，若是非绿地则去除所述绿地； 修正规则二:若多光谱影像提取结果为绿地，则检查Google Earth影像的提取结果中 是否有弱分类的绿地，若有，则将所述弱分类的绿地替换为强分类的绿地； 修正规则三:根据所述初始绿地图斑提取绿地图斑外边界，若所述外边界内包含的非 绿地面积大于阈值二，且所述多光谱影像提取结果为绿地，则对所述非绿地进行填充将其 归类为绿地。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法，其特 征在于，所述样本的尺寸为4000 X 4000像素或1000 X 1000像素。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法，其特 征在于，所述深度学习的方法为卷积神经网络算法。 4 .根据权利要求3所述的面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法，其特 征在于，所述卷积神经网络算法采用DlinkNet卷积神经网络算法或UNet卷积神经网络算 法。 5 .根据权利要求1所述的面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法，其特 征在于，所述多光谱影像通过Sentinel 2卫星获得。 6 .根据权利要求1所述的面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法，其特 征在于，计算所述月度归一化植被指数之后，还包括将所述月度归一化植被指数进行季节 时间序列特征分类的步骤。 7 .根据权利要求1所述的面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法，其特 征在于，根据所有月份的多光谱影像计算月度归一化植被指数，获得年度多光谱绿地图斑 时，根据季节性的归一化植被指数特征对所述多光谱影像进行分类，且分类时采用Random Forest算法。 8 .根据权利要求1所述的面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提取方法，其特 征在于，所述阈值一为一颗树的树冠面积;所述阈值二为一间房屋的大小。 9 .根据权利要求1-8中任意一项所述的面向图斑的高分辨率年度城市绿地遥感信息提 取方法，其特征在于，还包括根据所述修正后的年度绿地图斑，计算每个图斑内部时间序列 特征，获取面向图斑的绿地变化信息的步骤。
